reference_policy: official/primary sources first; stricter risk interpretation wins

references.title: GateZero Project Truth
references.why: Defines what the product is and is not; prevents bot/profit hype drift.

references.title: GateZero Product Wedge
references.why: Locks the product promise: no trade without evidence, no execution without risk approval.

references.title: GateZero Risk Rules
references.why: Hard safety constraints that outrank feature appetite.

references.title: Financial Risk Gates
references.why: Defines the allowed autonomy level and promotion/rollback path.

references.title: ISO 31000:2018 Risk Management Guidelines
references.why: Risk identification, analysis, treatment, monitoring, and communication principles.

references.title: NIST AI Risk Management Framework 1.0
references.why: Risk lifecycle framing for AI-assisted decision support.

references.title: CFTC AI Trading Bots Advisory
references.why: Reminds Risk Agent that AI cannot predict sudden market changes and high-return claims are suspicious.

references.title: IBKR Order Types / Notes & Limitations
references.why: Order-type constraints and execution caveats must feed risk review.

references.title: Google SRE Book - Embracing Risk
references.why: Useful for explicit risk budgets, although financial risk remains stricter than service-risk tolerance.
